name: blog_or_content_page_10_pages_recipes

observed_strengths.observation: Dedicated recipe page for product use-cases — 'Smoothies, protein shakes, lattes, and more all made with our coffee alternative' per meta description; recipe content builds habitual use and reduces churn by showing product versatility beyond the standard morning ritual

Meta description targets 'coffee alternative recipes' and 'how to enjoy MUD\WTR' queries — recipe content is a natural SEO surface for long-tail 'mudwtr latte recipe', 'adaptogenic smoothie' search queries that attract discovery-mode visitors

missing_or_weak.observation: H1 'SpecialtyRecipes' (concatenated text — likely a CSS image-replacement class that was read as heading text by the crawler) — the actual H1 visual content is a stylized image, not a text heading; this means the page has no text H1 for SEO purposes
No product purchase CTAs visible in page headings — recipe visitors who find specific product uses are not directed to buy the relevant product (e.g., 'Try this recipe with Hot Cacao → Add to cart')
No email capture heading visible on recipes page — content-engaged visitors who browse recipes are not captured for email nurture sequences
Only H1 present — no H2/H3 sections visible beyond navigation footer; the recipes page content structure may be image-heavy with little crawlable text, reducing SEO keyword density

purchase_path_notes: Recipe content is strong for retention and discovery but lacks in-page conversion infrastructure. Product CTAs inline with specific recipe steps (e.g., 'Made with Hot Cacao — shop now') would convert recipe-engaged visitors to buyers.
